zh.Exeter, Devonshire, England: 英國英格蘭戴文艾可謝特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Courtenay the younger於大約1348年出生在英國戴文艾可謝特時，她的父親Hugh Courtenay III46歲，母親Margaret de Bohun38歲。她和Sir Theobald Grenville II至少有1個兒子。她去世於1381年7月之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Courtenay the younger于大约1348年出生在英国戴文艾可謝特时，她的父亲Hugh Courtenay III46岁，母亲Margaret de Bohun38岁。她和Sir Theobald Grenville II至少有1个儿子。她去世于1381年7月之后。

nameMeanings.name: Courtenay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Courtney  .

variants.name: Courtney
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Courtenay near Sens in northern France, or some other place similarly named, from the name of a Gallo-Roman landlord, Curtenus (a derivative of Latin curtus ‘short’) + the locative suffix -acum.
Irish: English surname adopted for Ó Curnáin ‘descendant of Curnán’, an Old Irish personal name based on a diminutive of corn ‘horn’.

History: A family of this name (see 1 above) can be traced back to Athon de Courtenay, who lived in Courtenay in Loiret in the early 11th century. Renaud de Courtenay held land in Devon in the 12th century, and the family later held the title of Earls of Devon following the marriage of Hugh de Courtenay to Margaret de Bohun, granddaughter of King Edward I, in 1325.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Courtenay the younger was born about 1348, in Exeter, Devon, England, her father, Hugh Courtenay III, was 46 and her mother, Margaret de Bohun, was 38. She had at least 1 son with Sir Theobald Grenville II. She died after July 1381.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Courtenay the younger est née environ 1348, à Exeter, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Hugh Courtenay III, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Bohun, avait 38 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sir Theobald Grenville II. Elle est décédée après juillet 1381.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Courtenay the younger um 1348 in Exeter, Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh Courtenay III 46 und ihre Mutter Margaret de Bohun 38. Sie hatte mit Sir Theobald Grenville II mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb nach Juli 1381.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Courtenay the younger è nata il circa 1348 nel Exeter, Devon, England, sua padre, Hugh Courtenay III, aveva 46 anni e sua madre, Margaret de Bohun, aveva 38 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sir Theobald Grenville II. Morì dopo il luglio 1381.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Courtenay the younger(이)가 대략 1348년 영국 데폰 세터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Courtenay III(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Bohun(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 Sir Theobald Grenville II(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1381년 7월 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Courtenay the younger nasceu aproximadamente 1348, em Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh Courtenay III, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Bohun, tinha 38 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Sir Theobald Grenville II. Ela faleceu após julho de 1381.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Courtenay the younger родилась около 1348 г. в местности эксетир, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Hugh Courtenay III было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Bohun, 38. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir Theobald Grenville II. Она умерла после июля 1381 г.
